title: Why Shoals Technologies Group (SHLS) Is Up 6.2% After Hiking 2026 Revenue Guidance And Backlog Outlook
text: Shoals Technologies Group saw its stock rise following announcements that strengthened investor confidence in near-term growth prospects. The company elevated its full-year 2026 revenue guidance to a range of $600-640 million, up from previous expectations. Management highlighted a record US$758 million backlog and awarded orders, demonstrating robust underlying demand for its electrical balance of system solutions. First-quarter 2026 revenues reached $140.56 million, nearly double the prior year's $80.36 million, though the company remained near breakeven. The analyst notes that ownership of Shoals requires believing that utility scale solar and adjacent power markets will keep needing its EBOS and storage solutions. The upgraded outlook reinforces confidence in execution capability. The analyst identifies that the biggest risk, in my view, remains pressure on margins from higher costs and pricing, which the guidance update does not fully resolve. Legal and warranty cost pressures also remain a concern for profitability conversion.
